title: zieria arborescens subsp. decurrens
intro: Zieria arborescens subsp. decurrens, also known as the mountain pepper, is a subspecies of the Zieria arborescens plant, native to the eastern regions of Australia. The mountain pepper has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and has been used to treat conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
